commit.message:
Add daily analytics email report Lambda

New Lambda sends a daily email at 8 AM EST with:
- Yesterday's unique visitors and total page hits
- 7-day average with week-over-week trend (up/down/flat %)
- Top 10 most visited pages
- Top 5 external referrers
- Warnings: zero traffic, >30% drops, suspicious referrers, bot detection

Uses efficient DynamoDB sk range query (TIME#<ms> BETWEEN) to fetch
only the last 14 days instead of scanning all records. Single query,
partitioned in memory for yesterday/this-week/last-week analysis.

Dark-themed HTML email matching existing newsletter style.

Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)
via [Happy](https://happy.engineering)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: Happy <yesreply@happy.engineering>

live-build needs to mount /dev/pts and /proc inside chroot,
which requires privileged container access.

Matches the Figma design with custom header, 2-column tool grid, and proper icon styling. Reuses icon assets from main (icon_extract_audio, icon_speed, icon_merge, icon_clip). Adds onBack and onNavigateToHistory callbacks matching video/image converters.

feat: add dynamic data sources footer to analysis report

Backend tracks which tools executed, maps them to source metadata
(Yahoo Finance, Finnhub, Claude), deduplicates, and sends a
"sources" SSE event before the text stream. Frontend captures this
and renders clickable source links with tool breakdowns between
the markdown and the disclaimer footer.

feat(compliance): institutional positioning and legal compliance across all surfaces

Add explicit legal backing under four pillars (Tecnologicamente robusto,
Metodologicamente defensável, Juridicamente responsável, Publicamente auditável)
across web app, README, wiki and docs.

Web app:
- New /compliance page: headline, 4-pillar cards, full legal basis table
  with links to planalto.gov.br, what-we-dont-do section, compliance plan
- Implement SiteFooter (was return null): 4-column footer with Base Legal,
  Auditabilidade, Transparência columns + disclaimer bottom bar; rendered
  on every page via layout.tsx
- Home page: add "Posicionamento Institucional" strip between hero and nav
  with 4-pillar cards and prominent /compliance link
- Methodology: expand Base Legal table with direct planalto.gov.br links,
  add "Conformidade da Metodologia" section with 4 pillars and CTA

Docs:
- New docs/COMPLIANCE.md: full technical-legal document (8 sections:
  legal status, constitutional foundations, LAI, LGPD, compliance plan,
  what we dont do, contestation, editorial responsibility)
- New docs/wiki/Legal-Compliance.md: accessible wiki page with 4-pillar
  table, law references, quick FAQ and platform comparison
- docs/wiki/FAQ.md: +5 legal compliance Q&As (legality, LGPD, liability,
  who can use, how to contest)
- README.md: Compliance section with LAI/LGPD/AGPL-3.0 badges and 4-pillar
  table linking to docs/COMPLIANCE.md

All legal citations reference specific articles (no vague references).
Content accessible to citizens; technically precise for auditors/lawyers.

commit.message:
feat(governance): domain guard, veracity scoring, compliance automation & public /sources

- Add shared/connectors/domain_guard.py: government TLD whitelist (.gov.br,
  .leg.br, .jus.br, .mil.br, .mp.br, .def.br) enforced at HTTP layer;
  DomainException for controlled non-government sources; DomainNotAllowedError

- Add shared/connectors/veracity.py: SourceVeracityProfile (5-criterion
  weighted model: domain 40%, legal authority 25%, availability 15%, API docs
  10%, metadata 10%); SOURCE_VERACITY_REGISTRY for all 29 connector:job pairs

- Rewrite CEAPS fetch (senado.py): remove Codante dependency, use official
  Senado API legis.senado.leg.br/dadosabertos/senador/{id}/ceaps; per-senator
  cursor s{idx}y{idx}; XML/JSON dual-format support

- Integrate domain guard into http_client.py (_guarded_client helper); remove
  senado_ceaps_client(); add validate_domain call to querido_diario.py

- Add veracity columns to CoverageRegistry ORM + Alembic migration 0013
  (nullable ADD COLUMN — zero-downtime safe)

- Add worker/tasks/compliance_tasks.py: weekly check_source_compliance Celery
  task (Monday 06:00 UTC) — domain validation, exception expiry alerts, HTTP
  HEAD probes, coverage_registry status update

- Add @explanatory_only decorator (shared/ai/provider.py): asserts LLM
  functions return str, raises TypeError otherwise, logs every invocation;
  applied to explain_signal()

- Add GET /public/sources endpoint: full transparency on data provenance,
  veracity scores, domain whitelist and controlled exceptions

- Add docs/GOVERNANCE.md: source classification policy, veracity methodology,
  LLM usage policy, compliance automation, reproducibility guarantees

- Add guardrails to CLAUDE.md: domain guard mandatory, new sources need
  veracity profile + GOVERNANCE.md update, LLM must use @explanatory_only

- Add 43 new tests across domain_guard, veracity, public_sources,
  compliance_tasks, llm_guardrails; update Senado and scheduler tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: parent force toggles now turn off dependent sub-toggles

When a parent toggle is disabled, its dependents are now forcibly
unchecked and their physics state synced (not just visually disabled).
Covers all dependency chains: Gravity→GM, Gravity+Rel→1PN,
Coulomb→Magnetic, Relativity→Radiation/Spin-Orbit/Signal Delay,
BH→Signal Delay.

commit.message:
Dedup GOL correlati: rimuove pronostici GOL ridondanti/in conflitto

- 15 regole dedup per coppie GOL sulla stessa partita
- Over 2.5 + Goal → tieni Goal (83% HR vs 50%)
- Over 1.5 + Goal → tieni Over 1.5 (100% HR)
- Under 2.5 + Goal → tieni Under 2.5 (conflitto)
- Over + No Goal → tieni Over (previsione forte)
- Under vince su tutto (conservativo)
- Backtest: GOL 66.3% → 68.0% HR, P/L +4.60u → +8.15u (+3.55u)
- Totale: 67.2% → 68.2% HR, P/L +38.07u → +41.62u (+3.55u)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

sync and async agents. Includes guide and API reference docs.

commit.message:
Sanitize error responses to prevent Google API key leakage

Raw Google Sheets API errors were being returned to clients, exposing
the API key in error URLs. All error responses now go through _safe_error()
which strips sensitive details and logs the full error server-side.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Handle RunEvent::Reopen so clicking the dock icon shows and
focuses the main window when it was hidden via the red close button.

commit.message:
feat: add topology selector (torus, Klein bottle, projective plane)

When boundary mode is "loop", a new topology toggle appears with three
options: Torus (standard periodic), Klein bottle (y-wrap flips x), and
RP² (both axes flip). Topology-aware minimum-image separation replaces
all inline torus-wrap patterns across forces, potential, energy,
signal-delay, and collisions. Ghost generation handles velocity/spin
flips for non-orientable boundaries.

SCRUM-20: Performance optimization — GZip, LLM cache, DB singleton, indexes, metrics

- Add GZipMiddleware for response compression (>1KB payloads)
- Create LLM response cache with 24h TTL (Redis→TTLCache dual backend)
- Integrate LLM cache into all 4 agents (recommendation, review, price, policy)
- Convert DB engine + session factory to module-level singletons
- Add Alembic migration 003 with 4 new performance indexes (rating, stock, review product_id, review rating)
- Create P50/P95 latency metrics tracker with /health/metrics endpoint
- Add startup cache warmer hook
- Trim agent prompts ~30% for token reduction
- Add 15 new tests (7 LLM cache, 5 metrics, 3 DB singleton)
- Update ARCHITECTURE.md and DATABASE.md docs

feat: migration 10 — extract featuring artists from track titles

Add extract_feat_from_title() that parses patterns like (feat. X),
(ft. X), [featuring X], (avec X) from track titles and returns the
artist names. These are then linked in track_artists at scan time.

Migration 10 applies this retroactively to all existing tracks so
albums like "Titanium (feat. Sia)" show Sia in the featuring section
even when the ARTIST tag only contains "David Guetta".

feat: dynamic DMR talkgroup fetching from BrandMeister, TGIF, and RadioID

Adds a talkgroup fetch pipeline mirroring the RepeaterBook repeater pipeline.
Fills the radio's 10,000-slot DMR contact list (vs ~30 previously) and uses
actual per-repeater TS1/TS2 TG assignments from RadioID for home-tier channels
instead of hardcoded BrandMeister defaults.

New module: builder/talkgroups.py
- TalkgroupClient: fetches BrandMeister + TGIF registries, cached 7 days
- RadioIDClient: fetches per-repeater static TG lists by state, cached 7 days
- TalkgroupRegistry: merged lookup (name, call_type) with legacy tg_name() fallback

Updated files:
- builder/api.py: RepeaterBook cache TTL 12h → 30 days
- builder/models.py: add Talkgroup dataclass
- builder/zones.py: home_state_channels() uses RadioID TG data when available
- builder/codeplug.py: contact fill in priority order up to radio_max_tgs
- builder/build_config.py: add talkgroups config section (all on by default)
- builder/README.md: pipeline and API reference docs
- screens/build_screen.py: TG fetch steps in build pipeline; clear cache updated
- tool_discovery.py: add max_talkgroups field to RadioProfile (default 10,000)

Tests: 47 new tests in tests/test_talkgroups.py; 408 total passing.

Add critic calibration: approve-with-notes, resolution staging, convergence memory

Introduces three-way critic decision (approve / approve-with-notes / request-changes)
with resolution_stage on findings (plan vs implementation). Plan-stage blocking findings
gate retries; implementation-stage findings forwarded as guidance to IMPLEMENTER.

Key changes:
- New critic_semantics.py module for normalization/filtering helpers
- Normalization choke point in _run_critic() — invalid stages default to plan,
  implementation+blocking downgraded to warning
- Convergence memory: previous critic report passed on retry with anti-anchoring rules
- Decision enforcement: approve rejected if plan-stage blockers remain
- Implementer context includes grouped critic notes (capped at 20)
- 23 new tests in test_critic_calibration.py

commit.message:
Fix flyte-to-union prefix and add singleTenantOrgID to selfhosted DP (#269)

* Consolidate namespace_mapping into single canonical Helm value

The top-level `namespace_mapping` in values.yaml was dead config (no
template referenced `.Values.namespace_mapping`). Wire it as the
canonical source of truth that cascades to all dataplane services when
explicitly set:

- clusterresourcesync: falls back to `.Values.namespace_mapping` when
  `.Values.config.namespace_mapping` is not set
- nodeexecutor: auto-injects root-level `namespace_mapping` from the
  top-level value when `executor.raw_config.namespace_mapping` is not set
- propeller: falls back to top-level value when `config.namespace_config`
  is not set
- operator: falls back to top-level value for `org.namespaceTemplate`
  when `config.operator.org` is not set

Default remains empty (no template). Per-service overrides still take
precedence for backward compatibility.

towards RUN-102

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add namespace_mapping to test values to verify cascading

Add `namespace_mapping.template` to aws and fully-selfhosted test values
to verify the canonical value cascades correctly to all service configmaps
(clusterresourcesync, executor, propeller, operator).

Azure test values already set per-service overrides (`config.namespace_config`,
`config.operator.org`), which confirms backward compat (overrides take
precedence over the top-level value).

towards RUN-102

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Regenerate golden files for helm-test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Regenerate golden files after rebase onto main

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add namespace_mapping docs to controlplane values.yaml

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix flyte-to-union prefix and add singleTenantOrgID to selfhosted DP

Three fixes for selfhosted dataplane deployments:

1. ClusterRoleBinding `union-clustersync-resource` referenced ClusterRole
   `clustersync-resource` instead of `union-clustersync-resource`, causing
   all CRS namespace creation to fail with RBAC errors. Introduced in #260.

2. operator-proxy deployment referenced ConfigMap
   `flyte-clusterresourcesync-config` instead of
   `union-clusterresourcesync-config`, leaving the proxy stuck in
   ContainerCreating.

3. Add singleTenantOrgID to AWS and GCP selfhosted-intracluster dataplane
   values. Without this, the DP operator-proxy identity interceptor rejects
   internal requests from the CP dataproxy (401 on CreateUploadLocation).
   The CP already sets this — the DP was missing it.

NOTE: Existing deployments will need the old ClusterRoleBinding deleted
before ArgoCD can recreate it, since roleRef is immutable:
  kubectl delete clusterrolebinding union-clustersync-resource

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Regenerate golden files for helm-test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
multi-env vf-eval: switch between progress bars with arrow keys (#979)

* Add arrow-key navigation between env detail panels in vf-eval display

- Add key listener infrastructure to BaseDisplay (cbreak mode, stdin polling
  via os.read on fd 0, arrow key escape sequence parsing)
- Show single selected env detail panel instead of all running envs
- Left/right arrow keys cycle through envs; selected env highlighted in overview
- Overview capped at half terminal height with priority ordering when truncated
  (failed > running > completed > pending), selected env always visible
- Fix terminal size detection: use os.get_terminal_size(0) since stdout/stderr
  are redirected to pipes, making shutil.get_terminal_size() fall back to 24 lines
- Screen mode (--tui) adaptively sizes log panel to fill terminal
- Footer shows navigation hint when multiple envs present
- Stop key listener before wait_for_exit to prevent stdin contention

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Use daemon thread for key listener instead of asyncio task

The asyncio task couldn't run when the event loop was blocked by
synchronous env startup work (package installation, etc.), so arrow
key navigation only worked once all envs were running. A daemon thread
runs independently of the event loop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Improve overview panel: env counter in title, better selection indicator

- Show "(env n/x)" in detail panel title for multi-env evals
- Add ▶ prefix to selected env in overview for clearer indication
- Change running env icon from ▸ (triangle) to ● (filled circle)
  to pair with ○ (empty circle) for pending
- Add spacing between selection arrow and status icon

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Scroll overview panel to keep selected env visible

Replace priority-ordering with a sliding window that follows the
selected env. Shows "... N above" / "... and N more" indicators
when the list is truncated at top/bottom.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Revert unrelated AGENTS.md sync changes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix: only set cbreak mode when key listener is active

Move cbreak terminal setup from start() into _start_key_listener()
so that GEPADisplay (sync context manager, no key listener) doesn't
leave the terminal in cbreak mode with no thread consuming keystrokes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Revert "Fix: only set cbreak mode when key listener is active"

This reverts commit f8e3b8f45cc8702ddb84cb644f9ddcbadd93ec2e.

* Fix key listener busy-loop on stdin EOF

Break out of the loop when os.read returns empty bytes (EOF from
SSH disconnect, terminal close, etc.) instead of spinning at 100% CPU.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Adaptive log panel height in both screen and non-screen modes

Fill remaining terminal space with the log panel regardless of
display mode, instead of using a fixed 20-line default in non-screen.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Adaptive log panel height fills terminal in both display modes

Use terminal-adaptive log sizing in non-screen mode too (was fixed
at 20 lines). Includes 2-line buffer in detail_fixed to account for
Rich rendering overhead so the footer stays visible.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Measure actual rendered height for adaptive log panel sizing

Render content items to a temporary buffer to count real terminal
lines (accounting for text wrapping) instead of counting Python
objects. This ensures the log panel fills exactly the remaining
space regardless of metrics count or terminal width.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Document arrow-key navigation in multi-env evaluation display

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add log panel scrolling and wrapped line indentation

- Up/down arrow keys scroll through log history (3 lines per press)
- Log lines wrap with 4-space indented continuation lines for clarity
- Scroll offset shown in panel title; resets on env switch
- Footer hint updated to show scroll controls

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add colored log headers in eval display log panel

Parse log lines into timestamp, source, level, and message parts
with distinct styles. Level colors follow common conventions:
DEBUG=dim blue, INFO=bold green, WARNING=bold yellow, ERROR=bold red,
CRITICAL=bold red reverse. Timestamp is bold dim, source is dim cyan.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(research): variance reduction theorem — Var/Mean = 1/sqrt(5) EXACT

From det(M)=-1:
  J(φ) = -1/φ² → AR(1) with ρ = -1/φ²
  → Var/Mean = (1+ρ)/(1-ρ) = φ/(φ+2) = 1/√5

Algebraic proof in 4 lines. The discriminant disc=5 of Q(√5)
appears as the EXACT variance reduction factor from Poisson.

Two predictions from det=-1:
1. Var/Mean = 1/√5 (theorem, exact)
2. NumVar coeff = √5/φ⁵ ≈ 2/π² (conjecture, 99.5%)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
